strengths: Demonstrated proper traction setup with Maryland grasper positioning
Maintained steady cutting motion along most of the circle perimeter
Showed good instrument coordination during the cutting phases
weaknesses: Failed to complete the circle cut-out, leaving the marked area attached to the gauze
Did not achieve full liberation of the circular piece from the surrounding material
May have rushed the final cutting phase without ensuring complete separation

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, ensuring each quarter is fully separated before moving to the next.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the circle cut-out.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting tension throughout the entire circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Did not achieve full liberation of the circular piece.

drills.name: Complete separation verification drill
drills.description: Practice cutting circles with deliberate pauses to verify complete separation at multiple points before considering the task finished.
drills.targets_weakness: Rushed the final cutting phase without ensuring complete separation.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on task completion accuracy over speed - ensure the circle is fully liberated before considering the task finished. Your cutting technique shows promise but needs refinement in the final separation phase.
